Title: No loading indicator while results are being fetched

Finding:
In `render()`, the results container is cleared synchronously (`resultsEl.innerHTML = '';`) and then the route data is awaited. During that await there is no loading, skeleton, or 'Loading…' state — the results panel is simply blank. The status footer is intentionally left untouched (to preserve navigation targets), so on the first load the user sees an empty table and empty status with no indication that work is in progress. This hurts perceived performance and can look like a broken/empty UI, especially on slower connections or larger directories. This lifecycle is newly authored in `render-orchestrator.ts`.

In `src/app/render-orchestrator.ts`, inside the `render()` function, immediately after the line `resultsEl.innerHTML = '';` (and before the `try`/`await getApi().browse(...)` / `search(...)` calls), insert a synchronous loading placeholder into the results element, e.g. `resultsEl.textContent = 'Loading…';`. This text is naturally replaced on success by `renderBrowse(result)` / `renderSearch(result)` (which set `resultsEl.innerHTML = ''` then append the table) and on error by the existing `resultsEl.textContent = 'Error: ' + ...` branch, so no additional cleanup is required. Keep the existing race-guard token logic and the intentional non-clearing of the breadcrumb/status intact.
